codeReview.summary: The feature satisfies VAL-ONC-001. `src/providers/defillama.ts` exports DeFiLlama fetch helpers for pool data, token prices, and DEX volume aggregates, uses native fetch, reads `DEFILLAMA_BASE_URL` from config, and returns null on upstream failures while tests cover mocked success and failure paths.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The onchain worker followed the skill overall, but the transcript does not show the skill-mandated manual curl verification step for a valid and invalid request. This feature is provider-only, so the step may not be necessary for non-route work.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `.factory/skills/onchain-api-worker/SKILL.md` step 10 requires starting the API and verifying one valid plus one invalid curl request, while handoff `2026-03-27T03-24-35-662Z__defillama-provider-module__950e6bc7-a164-4923-96ba-f9aa5d2e2bb2.json` lists only test/typecheck/git-status verification and no interactive checks.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker handoff flagged a pre-existing README modification outside feature scope. This kind of ambient working-tree state can complicate commit attribution, but AGENTS currently only documents earlier uncommitted foundation work, not how later milestones should treat unrelated local doc edits.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff `2026-03-27T03-24-35-662Z__defillama-provider-module__950e6bc7-a164-4923-96ba-f9aa5d2e2bb2.json` notes `README.md` remained modified outside the feature scope; `AGENTS.md` documents foundation uncommitted work but has no parallel note for unrelated local modifications during later milestones.

summary: Pass. The DeFiLlama provider feature is implemented and tested as requested for VAL-ONC-001, with no code-level blocking or non-blocking defects found in this review. I noted two shared-state follow-ups: the onchain skill may over-require curl verification for provider-only tasks, and mission guidance could better address unrelated pre-existing working-tree changes during later milestones.
